free_text: A Re-assessment of Harwell mass spectrometric
measurements of the cross sections of Pu-239,
Pu-240 and Pu-241 for Maxwellian neutrons
Irradiation carried out in the no4 hobson-
honeycomb unit, at the end nearest the core.
The irradiation can was separated from the nearest
fuel rod by 15 cm aluminium alloy and 62 cm heavy
The neutron spectrum was Maxwellian with a
temperature of 116 degrees and an epithermal component
.Target materials were aliquots of mixtures of
highly enriched plutonium isotopes, in nitric acid
solution, supplied by the electromagnetic separation
group at Harwell. Mixtures were deposited on precipte
silica.Samples contained about 10 mg silica and about
10 micro-gram plutonium in small silica phials under
chemically by anion exchange column, then
analyzed for relative isotopic abundances by mass
Mass separation in A.E.I. Type MS5 mass
value taken as 37.5 +- 0.13 b, revised value
.The original analysis in JIN,28,2467 used an
interactive fitting procedure to obtain a self-
consistent set of cross sections from all fourteen
.The revised procedure considers the experiment in
tree separate parts, with each isotopic mixture
.Errors are given at one standard deviation
* uncertainties in other assumed quantities such
as the Pu-241 half life etc.
